title: Gentle Croatian Oxtail Stew
description: This hearty stew features tender oxtail and earthy vegetables in a rich broth, perfect for a cold winter's night. The dish is shojin-friendly and can be served with a side of crusty bread or over mashed potatoes.
instructions: Heat oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the oxtail and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the oxtail from the pot and set it aside.
Add the onion to the pot and cook until it is softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the carrots and potatoes to the pot and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Add the mushrooms to the pot and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add the vegetable broth, tomato paste, thyme, salt, and pepper to the pot and stir to combine.
Return the oxtail to the pot and bring the stew to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, until the oxtail is tender, about 2 1/2 hours.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
